it follows that the action taken by the Special Court to 
punish the appellant for contempt was valid. The Special 
Court has only faulted in being unduly lenient in awarding 
the sentence. We do not think it is necessary, under the

Shanmugam @ Lakshminarayanan v. High Court of Madras
circumstances, to examine the finding of the Special Court 
that this was a continuing wrong or contempt and, therefore, 
action for contempt was not barred by Section 20.”
14. While the appellants have referred to para 44 of “Pallav Sheth”(supra), 
the respondent has relied upon paragraph nos. 30, 41 & 42. Upon 
reading of the entire judgment in the matter of “Pallav Sheth” (supra), 
it is clearly depicted that the contempt action must be initiated either 
by filing of an application or by the Court issuing notice suo motu 
within a period of one year from the date on which the contempt is
